title: Platinum Thinpack
wikitext:
[]: Platinum "Thinpack"             Quote Post by Rahaquiel-2D1S » Sat Oct 28, 2006 7:45 am Is this it? If not, just tell me the title I should look for and maybe a link or something.

[]:             Quote Post by jcmoorehead » Sat Oct 28, 2006 7:50 am Yup thats the one.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Sat Oct 28, 2006 8:03 am It probably is but don't buy it from that site. Online you can buy it from Amazon for $67 and I know it is cheaper than that in store at Best Buy.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 28, 2006 11:08 am According to the number of discs, upc, and general price, it is the thinpak. But like hunter said, there are better places to buy it. I know a lot of people who have bought the set for as low as $50. Check around the various well known dealers, and find a good deal. Preliminary search reveals that RightStuf is selling it for $48.99. You can't go wrong with that deal! http://www.rightstuf.com/cgi-bin/catalogmgr/-HzmuhlqvMfJDuxrLt/browse/item/67745/4/0/0

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Oct 28, 2006 1:40 pm That appears to be what I have. Be warned: the image quality is horrible and it's interlaced to hell and back.

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Sun Oct 29, 2006 4:42 pm i got mine from amazon for 45 + shipping

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Oct 29, 2006 9:22 pm I know someone who bought the entire 7 set Platinum Region 2 edition and got nothing but 26 episodes of pure telecine. It was pretty painful. Order Region 1 editions only .

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 01, 2006 9:49 am BobBQ wrote: That appears to be what I have. Be warned: the image quality is horrible and it's interlaced to hell and back.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Nov 01, 2006 11:04 am Then where the fork do all these AMV editors get their super-crispy video from? That can't all be interlacing!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Nov 01, 2006 11:20 am Region 1 video sources? Secret Ninja Techniques of Interlacing Removal?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Nov 01, 2006 1:19 pm www.play-asia.com

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Wed Nov 01, 2006 5:06 pm BobBQ wrote: Then where the fork do all these AMV editors get their super-crispy video from? That can't all be interlacing!
